scenario: An insurance-quote Form shows the customer's monthlyPremium as a read-only computed field -- the value derives from other Form fields (coverageAmount, term, age) and updates as the user changes those inputs. The customer can't edit monthlyPremium directly; it's a calculated display.
question: Which Form configuration fits a "read-only computed field that updates live"?

options.text: Use a Number Input component configured with disabled: true to display the computed order total as a read-only field -- this is a visually similar approach using the wrong element type. A disabled Number Input shows the value at form initialisation time but does not reactively update as the user changes quantity fields during the form session. The Text View element with a FEEL expression binding is the canonical pattern for live computed values in Camunda Forms. Text View evaluates its FEEL expression reactively as referenced input values change, displaying the updated computed total in real time.

Server-side pre-computation happens once before the form session starts and cannot respond to changes the user makes to quantity inputs during the form. The requirement is for the displayed total to update interactively as the user adjusts quantities. A Text View element with a FEEL expression provides client-side reactive calculation that updates the displayed value each time a referenced input field changes.

Camunda Forms are FEEL-based declarative components that abstract above direct JavaScript manipulation. Custom JavaScript code has no place in Camunda Form definitions. The declarative equivalent is using a Text View element with a FEEL expression binding that references the quantity inputs -- the Forms engine evaluates the FEEL expression reactively as input values change, providing live calculation without any imperative code.

md:7-13). md:11-17 ("You can use any valid FEEL expression within these double braces"). The form re-evaluates the expression/template as bound variables change, so the displayed value updates live. Text view is read-only by definition (no input).

a.text: Incorrect. Camunda Forms support FEEL templating in display components like Text View. The expression references other form fields by their key (which mirrors the variable name); as the user edits those fields, the form recalculates the FEEL and updates the displayed value in real time. Read-only is intrinsic to Text View (it's a display, not an input). This is the canonical "live computed field" pattern in Camunda Forms.

b.text: Incorrect. A disabled Number Input shows a value but doesn't typically auto-update from other fields; it's bound to a static variable. Achievable with extra wiring, but Text View + FEEL is the direct path.

c.text: Incorrect. Pre-computing means the value is fixed at task activation; user changes to inputs wouldn't reflect.

d.text: Correct. Forms intentionally abstract above raw JS for portability across renderers.

explanation: Forms intentionally abstract above raw JS for portability across renderers.
